items.text:
Subject: Volunteer DJs Needed at WKUR Campus Radio

Dear Students,

WKUR Campus Radio is recruiting volunteer DJs and show hosts for the fall broadcast schedule, which begins September 8. No prior radio experience is required — training sessions will be held in the Media Building, Room 214, every Monday and Wednesday from 4:00 PM to 5:30 PM, starting August 24.

Volunteers who complete the two-week training and host at least one weekly show receive a $25 stipend per semester to help cover transportation, plus early access to concert tickets given away through the station. New hosts must also submit a short audio sample by August 20 so the program director can assign an appropriate time slot.

Please note that all volunteers are required to attend a one-hour equipment orientation before their first broadcast; students who miss this session won't be permitted to go on air until they complete a rescheduled orientation. If you're interested, stop by the station office in Media 210 or reply to this email with your available days.

We hope to see many new faces at the station this semester.

Best,
Marcus Chen
Program Director, WKUR Campus Radio

format_metadata.subject: Volunteer DJs Needed at WKUR Campus Radio

questions.stem: According to the email, what must new hosts submit by August 20?
questions.explanation: The email states that "new hosts must also submit a short audio sample by August 20 so the program director can assign an appropriate time slot."

questions.stem: What can be inferred about volunteers who miss the equipment orientation session?

options.A: They cannot go on air until they complete a rescheduled orientation.
options.B: They will lose their stipend for the rest of the semester.
options.C: They must reapply for the volunteer program the following year.
options.D: They are automatically assigned to a later broadcast slot.

questions.explanation: The email says students who miss the orientation "won't be permitted to go on air until they complete a rescheduled orientation," so they are barred from broadcasting until that happens.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To advertise concert tickets available to students
options.B: To recruit volunteer DJs and explain the training process
options.C: To announce a change in the campus radio broadcast schedule
options.D: To request additional funding for the radio station's equipment

questions.explanation: The email is recruiting volunteer DJs and hosts, and explains the training schedule, stipend, audio sample submission, and orientation requirement needed to join.

items.text:
@jordan.builds

Just shipped the beta of StudyStack, the flashcard app I built for our intro chemistry class, and I honestly can't stop refreshing the download count!! 🎉

The idea started last semester when I kept losing my paper flashcards before finals. StudyStack lets you scan a page of notes, and it automatically generates a flashcard deck, then quizzes you using spaced repetition so the cards you miss most show up more often. I spent most of winter break coding the scanning feature, and it still messes up on messy handwriting sometimes, but overall it's working way better than I expected.

Right now it's free for anyone with a .edu email, and I'm hoping to add a shared-deck feature next month so study groups can build one deck together instead of everyone making their own. If you try it, please send me feedback — I read every message, and the app is genuinely better because of comments from the last 200 people who tested it.

What feature should I build next? Drop your ideas below! #StudentDev #StudyStack

questions.stem: According to the post, what does StudyStack do when a user scans a page of notes?

options.A: It translates the notes into a study guide format.
options.B: It saves the images to a shared class folder.
options.C: It automatically generates a flashcard deck from them.
options.D: It emails a summary of the notes to the user.

questions.explanation: The post states that StudyStack "lets you scan a page of notes, and it automatically generates a flashcard deck."

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this post?

options.A: To criticize existing flashcard apps on the market
options.B: To share the launch of a student-built flashcard app and ask for feedback
options.C: To announce a partnership with the chemistry department
options.D: To promote a paid subscription plan for premium features

questions.explanation: The writer announces the beta launch of StudyStack, describes how it works, and asks followers to send feedback and suggest new features.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the last 200 people who tested the app?

options.A: They were required to pay a testing fee before using the app.
options.B: They will receive early access to the shared-deck feature.
options.C: Their feedback influenced changes to the app.
options.D: They mostly requested new features for other subjects.

questions.explanation: The writer says the app "is genuinely better because of comments from the last 200 people who tested it," implying their feedback shaped improvements.
